ai.hero_subtext: Learn how Aetna plans may cover addiction and mental-health treatment, and find your next steps.
ai.intro_html:
If you have Aetna health insurance, you may have coverage for addiction and substance-use treatment—including counseling, medication, and inpatient or outpatient care. Recovery Dawn is here to help you understand what your plan covers and connect you with treatment options that accept your insurance.

We're a free informational and referral service, not an insurance company or treatment provider. We can help you verify your benefits and explore what's available to you.

feature_points.heading: Verify your Aetna coverage
feature_points.text: We help you understand what addiction and mental-health services your Aetna plan may cover, including deductibles and out-of-pocket costs specific to your plan.

feature_points.heading: Find treatment options
feature_points.text: Once you know your benefits, we connect you with treatment providers and programs that accept your Aetna insurance.

feature_points.heading: Free, confidential support
feature_points.text: Our service is completely free and confidential—we're here to answer your questions and help you take the next step.

faq.q: Does Aetna cover addiction treatment?
faq.a: Many Aetna plans include coverage for addiction and substance-use treatment, but benefits vary by plan, employer, and state. We can help you check your specific plan's coverage for counseling, medication, detox, and inpatient or outpatient care.

faq.q: How do I find out what my Aetna plan covers?
faq.a: Contact Recovery Dawn and we'll help you verify your benefits. You can also call the customer service number on your Aetna insurance card to ask about addiction and mental-health treatment coverage.

faq.q: Will I have to pay out of pocket?
faq.a: Out-of-pocket costs depend on your individual Aetna plan—including deductibles, copays, and coinsurance. We recommend verifying these details with Aetna directly or through your plan documents before you start treatment.

faq.q: What if I'm in crisis right now?
faq.a: If you're having a mental-health or addiction emergency, call or text 988 (Suicide and Crisis Lifeline) or call 911. These services are available 24/7 and don't require insurance.

ai.local_note: Recovery Dawn serves people with Aetna coverage nationwide. To connect with treatment in your area and verify your specific plan benefits, reach out to us today—we're here to help.

ai.hero_subtext: Understand how your Ambetter plan can help you access addiction and mental-health treatment.
ai.intro_html:
If you have an Ambetter health plan and are looking for help with substance use or addiction, your coverage may include treatment options. Recovery Dawn is here to help you understand your benefits and find resources that work for you—at no cost.

This guide covers what Ambetter plans typically include for addiction treatment, how to verify your specific coverage, and next steps to connect with care. Remember: your benefits depend on your individual plan, so it's important to check directly with Ambetter or your plan documents.

feature_points.heading: Verify your Ambetter benefits
feature_points.text: Contact Ambetter directly or review your plan documents to confirm what addiction and mental-health treatment your plan covers, including inpatient, outpatient, and medication-assisted options.

feature_points.heading: Understand your costs upfront
feature_points.text: Ask Ambetter about deductibles, copays, and out-of-pocket maximums for treatment—your plan's cost-sharing varies, so get specifics before you start care.

feature_points.heading: Find in-network providers
feature_points.text: Ambetter can provide a list of in-network treatment facilities and providers; using in-network care typically lowers your out-of-pocket costs.

faq.q: Does Ambetter cover addiction treatment?
faq.a: Many Ambetter plans include coverage for substance-use treatment, but benefits vary by plan type and state. You'll need to verify your specific coverage by contacting Ambetter directly with your plan details.

faq.q: How do I check what my Ambetter plan covers?
faq.a: Call the member services number on your Ambetter insurance card, or log into your online account to review your plan's benefits summary. Have your plan documents ready so you can ask about inpatient rehab, outpatient programs, medication-assisted treatment, and behavioral health services.

faq.q: Will Ambetter cover outpatient or inpatient treatment?
faq.a: Coverage depends on your individual plan and may include both options, but the level of coverage (copays, deductibles, length of stay) varies. Verify your plan's details with Ambetter to understand what's covered.

faq.q: What if I'm in crisis right now?
faq.a: If you're in immediate danger, call 911. For addiction or mental-health support anytime, call or text 988 (Suicide and Crisis Lifeline)—it's free and confidential. Recovery Dawn can help you understand coverage once you're safe, but crisis support comes first.

ai.local_note: Recovery Dawn serves people nationwide. Whether you have Ambetter coverage or another plan, we can help you understand your benefits and connect you with resources in your area at no cost.

faq.q: Does my AmeriHealth plan cover addiction treatment?

faq.q: What types of treatment does AmeriHealth typically cover?
faq.a: Coverage may include inpatient and outpatient rehab, medication-assisted treatment, counseling, and mental-health services. The exact benefits depend on your individual plan. We'll help you understand what's available to you.

faq.q: How do I know if a treatment provider accepts my AmeriHealth plan?
faq.a: When you contact us, we connect you with providers and facilities in your area that work with AmeriHealth plans. You can also call your plan's customer service number (on your insurance card) to ask about in-network options.

ai.hero_subtext: Learn how Anthem may cover addiction and mental-health treatment, and find resources that work for you.

feature_points.text: Call the member services number on your Cigna card or log into your online account to confirm what addiction and mental-health treatment services are covered under your plan.

feature_points.text: Cigna offers different plan types — HMO, PPO, EPO, and others — and each may have different coverage rules, in-network providers, referral requirements, and out-of-pocket costs.

faq.q: Does Cigna cover addiction treatment?
faq.a: Most Cigna plans include coverage for addiction and substance-use treatment, but coverage varies by plan, employer, and state. You'll need to verify your specific benefits by calling the member services number on your insurance card or checking your plan documents.

faq.q: What types of treatment might be covered?
faq.a: Cigna plans typically cover detoxification, inpatient and outpatient rehab, medication-assisted treatment, counseling, and mental-health services. The exact services and how much you pay depends on your plan — always confirm your benefits before treatment starts.

faq.q: Will I need a referral for treatment?

faq.q: How do I verify my benefits with HealthPartners?
faq.a: Call the customer service number on your insurance card, or log into your HealthPartners member account online. Have your member ID ready and ask specifically about coverage for addiction treatment, mental-health services, and what your deductible and copays are.

faq.q: What types of treatment might be covered?
faq.a: Depending on your plan, HealthPartners may cover inpatient residential treatment, outpatient programs, counseling, therapy, and medication-assisted treatment. Your plan documents will list covered services—ask HealthPartners to clarify which options are available to you.

faq.q: Will HealthPartners cover my treatment if I'm in crisis?
faq.a: If you're in immediate crisis, call or text 988 (Suicide and Crisis Lifeline) or call 911. Emergency services are typically covered regardless of plan details, but verify coverage status with HealthPartners after you're safe.

ai.local_note: Recovery Dawn serves people nationwide with free information about addiction and mental-health coverage—we're not affiliated with HealthPartners or any insurance company. We help you understand your benefits and find treatment options that match your plan.

ai.hero_subtext: Understanding Highmark coverage for addiction and mental-health treatment.

Recovery Dawn is here to help you understand what your Highmark coverage may include and connect you with treatment options. We're a free informational and referral service, not an insurance company or treatment provider. We'll walk you through the next steps so you can get the care you need.

faq.q: How do I find out what my Highmark plan covers?
faq.a: Call the member services number on your Highmark insurance card and ask about addiction treatment, substance-use disorder services, and mental-health benefits. Have your plan details ready, and ask about deductibles, copays, and whether specific providers or treatment types are covered.

faq.a: Tricare plans typically include coverage for substance-use and mental-health treatment, but the specifics—including which providers, levels of care, and costs—depend on your individual plan. You'll need to verify your benefits directly with Tricare or your member services to understand what's covered.

faq.q: What types of treatment might Tricare cover?
faq.a: Tricare may cover inpatient treatment, outpatient programs, medication-assisted treatment, counseling, and related services. The exact coverage and any limitations depend on your plan type and circumstances. Contact Tricare to confirm what's available to you.

faq.q: How do I find a provider who accepts Tricare?
faq.a: You can search Tricare's provider directory online, call Tricare member services for referrals, or ask your primary-care provider for a recommendation. Recovery Dawn can also help you explore treatment options and understand how to use your benefits.

faq.q: Do I need a referral to start treatment?
faq.a: It depends on your specific Tricare plan. Some plans require a referral or prior authorization before treatment begins. Contact Tricare or your member services to find out what's required for your coverage.

ai.local_note: Tricare serves active-duty service members, retirees, and their families nationwide. To understand your coverage and find treatment options in your area, verify your benefits with Tricare and connect with Recovery Dawn for free guidance.

ai.hero_subtext: Understand your Tufts Health plan's coverage for addiction and mental-health treatment.
ai.intro_html:
If you have a Tufts Health plan, you may have coverage for addiction and substance-use treatment—including counseling, medication, and inpatient or outpatient care. Recovery Dawn helps you understand what your specific plan covers and connects you with treatment options that work with your benefits.

We're a free informational and referral service, not a treatment provider or insurance company. Our goal is to make it easier to get the care you need without confusion about costs or coverage.

feature_points.heading: Learn your coverage
feature_points.text: We'll help you understand what addiction and mental-health services your Tufts Health plan includes.

feature_points.heading: Verify your benefits
feature_points.text: You'll know your deductible, copay, and out-of-pocket limits before you reach out to treatment providers.

feature_points.heading: Find treatment options
feature_points.text: We connect you with providers and facilities in your area that accept your plan.

faq.q: Does my Tufts Health plan cover addiction treatment?
faq.a: Many Tufts Health plans do cover substance-use and addiction treatment, but coverage varies by plan type and employer. We can help you check your specific benefits. Call us to verify what's included in your plan.

faq.q: What types of treatment might be covered?
faq.a: Tufts Health plans typically cover options like counseling and therapy, medication-assisted treatment, outpatient programs, and inpatient or residential care—but exact coverage depends on your individual plan. We'll help you understand what applies to you.

faq.q: How do I know my copay or deductible for treatment?
faq.a: Your plan documents or member ID card will show your copay and deductible. You can also call the number on your card to speak with Tufts Health directly, or contact us and we'll help you get those details.

faq.q: Is Recovery Dawn part of Tufts Health?
faq.a: No. Recovery Dawn is an independent, free informational and referral service. We're not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by Tufts Health or any insurance company.

ai.local_note: Coverage and treatment options vary by location and plan. We'll help you find providers near you that accept your Tufts Health plan.

ai.hero_subtext: Find out how your UnitedHealthcare plan covers addiction and mental-health treatment.
ai.intro_html:
If you have a UnitedHealthcare plan and are looking for addiction or mental-health treatment, your coverage may help pay for services. Recovery Dawn is a free informational service that helps you understand your benefits and find treatment options that work for you.

The details of what's covered depend on your specific plan, employer, and state. We'll help you verify your benefits and connect you with next steps—no cost to you.

feature_points.heading: Verify your UnitedHealthcare benefits
feature_points.text: We help you understand what addiction and mental-health treatment your plan may cover, including deductibles, copays, and in-network providers.

feature_points.heading: Find treatment options near you
feature_points.text: Once you know your coverage, we connect you with treatment providers and resources in your area.

feature_points.heading: Free, confidential support
feature_points.text: Recovery Dawn is a free service—we're not a treatment provider, insurance company, or broker, and everything you share stays private.

faq.q: Does my UnitedHealthcare plan cover addiction treatment?
faq.a: Most UnitedHealthcare plans do cover some addiction and substance-use treatment, but coverage varies by plan type, employer, and state. We'll help you contact UnitedHealthcare directly to verify your specific benefits, including what types of treatment are covered and any out-of-pocket costs you may owe.

faq.q: What types of treatment might be covered?
faq.a: Depending on your plan, coverage may include detox, inpatient rehab, outpatient programs, medication-assisted treatment, and counseling. The exact services covered and your costs depend on your individual plan—we'll help you get those details from UnitedHealthcare.

faq.q: How do I check my UnitedHealthcare coverage?
faq.a: You can call the customer service number on your insurance card, log into your online account, or contact us and we'll guide you through the process. Be ready to ask about deductibles, copays, in-network providers, and any pre-approval requirements for treatment.

faq.q: Is Recovery Dawn free to use?
faq.a: Yes, Recovery Dawn is completely free. We're an informational and referral service, not a treatment provider or insurance company. We help you understand your benefits and find treatment options—there's no cost to you.

ai.local_note: Coverage details vary by UnitedHealthcare plan, employer, and location. Contact your plan directly or work with Recovery Dawn to verify your specific benefits before starting treatment.
